---
title: "Matching and Weighting Learning Resources"
---

## What This Website Is For

This website is a training and reference space for design-stage causal inference using matching and weighting methods in observational evaluation.

Its core goal is to show how to design, diagnose, and estimate observational comparisons responsibly using:

- exact matching
- coarsened exact matching
- entropy balancing for weighting

The material is organized around a practical evaluator workflow rather than a software-first workflow. The emphasis throughout the site is that:

- matching and weighting are design tools, not substitutes for causal assumptions
- balance must be assessed together with retained sample size and overlap
- entropy balancing should be treated as a weighting method, not as a matching method

The intended audience is evaluators, analysts, and staff who need a practical introduction to quasi-experimental design choices in programme and policy evaluation. The framing is especially aligned with training and capacity-building use cases, where methods need to be technically sound, explainable, and auditable.

## How The Site Is Structured

The site is split into three main areas:

- `Reports`: longer methods write-ups and their supporting audit materials
- `Additional Labs`: worked examples that extend the core material to other datasets and teaching scenarios
- `Slides`: presentation decks that support training delivery and lab handoff

If you are new to the site, the recommended sequence is:

1. Start with the main report.
2. Use the full-code and QA pages when you want implementation detail or audit traceability.
3. Move into the labs for additional worked examples.
4. Use the slide decks when you want a delivery-ready teaching version.

## Reports

The main report bundle is:

- [Matching Methods Report](report/matching_methods_report.qmd)
- [Notes on *The Effect*: Matching](report/effectbook_matching_notes.qmd)
- [Full Code Companion](report/matching_methods_report_full_code.qmd)
- [QA Log](report/matching_methods_report_qa_log.qmd)

## Dataset Expansion Labs

The dataset expansion workstream provides additional labs and teaching aids that apply the same design-first logic to other examples:

- [Dataset Expansion Lab Hub](labs/index.qmd)
- [Black Politicians Lab](labs/black_politicians_lab.qmd)
- [NSW + CPS Benchmark Lab](labs/nsw_cps_benchmark_lab.qmd)
- [Titanic Teaching Lab](labs/titanic_teaching_lab.qmd)

## Slides Space

A dedicated slides section supports training delivery, facilitator-led sessions, and report-to-lab transitions:

- [Slides Hub](slides/index.qmd)
- [Matching and Weighting Training Deck](slides/matching_methods_slides.qmd)
- [From Report to Labs: Background Lecture](slides/pre_lab_background_lecture.qmd)
